title: Maternity Homes - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Maternity Homes 9 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY AND CENTRAL COAST FATHER TAAFFE HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides maternity homes and transitional housing services. 3737 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Pregnant or parenting women ages 13-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (503) 508-3030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANDMA'S HOUSE OF CENTRAL OREGON GRANDMA'S HOUSE MATERNITY HOME SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Emergency and transitional shelter for pregnant and parenting youth and their children who are experiencing homeless or at risk of being homeless. Eligibility: Houseless youth ages 12-24 who are pregnant, experienced a miscarriage, interested in adoption, or have a child under the age of 5 Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Shelter: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 383-3515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES TRANSITIONAL LIVING MATERNITY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides long-term supportive housing, including case management, parenting and life skills support, access to healthcare, prenatal services, childcare resources, and community programs to help young parents move toward stability and independence. Participants must be willing to voluntarily engage in the program, follow house guidelines, and work with staff on goals related to stability, parenting, education, or employment. 115 N 10th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Women ages 18-24 who are pregnant or parenting and experiencing homelessness, at risk of homelessness, or living in unsafe or unstable housing Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 882-2053 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MAGDALENE HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Full-time residential care for pregnant and parenting young mothers and their children. Provides life skills training and access to essential services. Assists residents in applying for TANF, OHP, SNAP (food stamps), and other needed services. All furnishings are provided, including bedding. Donations of clothing are made available to residents and their children. 12 Black Oak Drive Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Female, pregnant and/or parenting, and between the ages of 14-24. Residents must attend school or otherwise progress in their education or work. Residents within the age range may stay as long as they wish, provided they follow Magdalene Home's rules for wholesome living. Residents choosing adoption may stay for up to 6 months following the adoption. Mothers who are under the guidance of DHS Child Welfare are welcome. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 773-5040 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides stable, secure, and nurturing residential care in a home environment for pregnant women and women with children. Residents may stay at any point through their pregnancy and stay up to approximately one year after the birth of their baby. Residents have access to additional support with gaining OB/GYN care, high school/GED education completion, gaining employment, budgeting, individual counseling, advocacy and case management, developing parenting skills, and support working towards family reunification. Participation in faith-based worship service of choosing is required. Accepts mothers with one additional child, when capacity allows. Does not accept couples or individuals with pets of any kind. 2850 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard #55 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Pregnant women age 18 or older who are in crisis (homeless, fleeing domestic violence, etc.). Women can be pregnant and have an additional child under the age of 3 depending on program capacity. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 648-4227 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME ROSEBURG SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter, food, and safety. Individualized care plans created for participants to support transition into independent living. 445 NE Chestnut Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Women 18 years and older who are pregant or who have an infant or child under the age of 3. Hours: Office hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-2pm, other hours by appointment Maternity home: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 464-2740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE MATERNITY HOME Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a faith-based maternity home. Women and their babies may stay in the program up to a year after the child is born, if working towards independence (school or job for at least 20 hours per week, doing chores). Eligibility: Pregnant women or parenting a child under the age of 1, between the ages of 18-25. Hours: Maternity home: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 415-7763 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITIONAL YOUTH YOUTH DEVELOPMENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supervised home for pregnant and parenting teens. Assists homeless teen mothers and their children by providing a supportive environment and teaching life skills to become self-sufficient while completing their high school education or equivalent. Eligibility: Pregnant and parenting females ages 18-24 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 350-7215 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC COMMUNITY SERVICES OF THE MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY AND CENTRAL COAST FATHER TAAFFE HOMES INTEGRAL YOUTH SERVICES TRANSITIONAL LIVING MATERNITY PROGRAM MAGDALENE HOME MAGDALENE HOME ROAD TO HOPE MATERNITY HOUSING AND SUPPORT SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME ROSEBURG SAFE HAVEN MATERNITY HOME
